summary: The q5 dataset shows uniform activity across all ten problems (q1-q10), each carrying exactly 100 issues with no proof status differentiation provided. The lack of variance and absence of resolution metadata makes it impossible to distinguish traction or solvability from the supplied data alone; richer per-issue metadata is required for meaningful prioritization.
problems: All ten problems (q1-q10) report an identical 100 issues each, indicating either a placeholder/default value or an uninstrumented counter rather than real differentiated activity
No proof status breakdown (open, closed, in-progress, verified) is available, so completion or solvability cannot be assessed
No per-problem traction signals (recent commits, contributor counts, comment velocity) are present to identify hotspots
The uniform issue count suggests potential data ingestion or aggregation error that should be validated before drawing conclusions
highlights: Full coverage: every problem in the dataset has tracked issues, indicating complete enumeration
Consistent baseline of 100 issues per problem provides a clean reference point once real metrics are layered in
Dataset is well-structured for a follow-up enrichment pass to add status and activity dimensions

suggested_todos.title: Audit the issue-counting pipeline to confirm whether the uniform 100-per-problem figure is real or a placeholder/default
Add proof status categorization (open, in-progress, verified, closed) to each problem to enable solvability assessment
Instrument per-problem activity metrics (recent edits, contributors, comment velocity) to surface true traction leaders
Re-run the insight report once enriched metadata is available to identify near-term solvable problems
Establish a baseline dashboard comparing q5 against other datasets to detect anomalies in issue distribution
